title: AutoRedTeam-LLM: Building an Automated Security Benchmark for Open-Source LLMs
subtitle: How I built a fully local red-teaming pipeline that attacks, scores, and defends three 7–8B models on an RTX 4060 — no API keys, no cloud compute, no manual annotation.
excerpt: Open-source LLMs are deployed in production without systematic safety evaluation. I built AutoRedTeam-LLM to automate the attack-classify-defend loop end to end — 150 adversarial prompts, 3 models, 3 defenses, zero human annotation, recording an overall 81.1% ASR across 450 evaluations.

title: Agentic Network Forensics: Automated PCAP Analysis at Scale
subtitle: A Multi-Engine, LLM-Augmented Pipeline for Large-Scale Network Threat Investigation
excerpt: Modern network forensic investigations face a scaling problem: packet captures from real incidents routinely span gigabytes across dozens of files, with millions of packets that no analyst can manually review in a reasonable timeframe.

title: SMS Phishing Detection with Multi-Agent AI: Building a Personal Anti-Scam Analyst
subtitle: How a Pipeline of Specialized Agents Tears Apart a Phishing SMS, From Message Text to Website Metadata
excerpt: What if the judgment didn't have to be human? A multi-agent AI system that analyses a suspicious SMS from every angle simultaneously, message text, URL structure, website content, HTTP headers, and domain registration history, each handled by its own specialised agent.
